chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Constitutional Petition No. D-457 of 2026 (Ali Jan versus Province of Sindh & others) Date Order with signature of Judge Before:- Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi 1. For order on office objection 2. For hearing of CMA No.1638/2026 3. For hearing of main case Date of hearing and order:-29.4.2026 Mr. Israr Ahmed Abro advocate for the petitioner Mr. Abdul Jalil Zubedi, Additional AG Mr. Allah Ditto Khoso, Litigation Officer, SE&LD --------------------- O R D E R Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. The petitioner, Ali Jan, has invoked the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, seeking directions to the respondents not to interfere with his service, contending that he was lawfully appointed under the deceased quota and is entitled to continue in employment. He has further prayed that the respondents be restrained from taking any illegal or adverse action against him during the pendency of the petition, and that they be directed to issue his official identification so that his salary may be released from the date of his joining. 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner was appointed as a Junior Clerk (BPS-11) in the Education Department against the deceased quota and duly submitted his joining report; however, despite rendering service, his salary has been withheld since the date of his appointment without lawful justification. 3. At this stage, learned Assistant Advocate General (AAG) submits that the matter may appropriately be referred to the Education Department for verification and scrutiny of the petitioner’s appointment and all ancillary issues.

Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioner was appointed as a Junior Clerk (BPS-11) in the Education Department against the deceased quota and duly submitted his joining report; however, despite rendering service, his salary has been withheld since the date of his appointment without lawful justification. 3. At this stage, learned Assistant Advocate General (AAG) submits that the matter may appropriately be referred to the Education Department for verification and scrutiny of the petitioner’s appointment and all ancillary issues. He further states that, if upon such verification the petitioner’s appointment is found to be genuine and in accordance with the deceased quota policy, as well as in conformity with any relevant orders of the Honourable Federal Constitutional Court, the case of the petitioner shall be processed for release of his salary within a period of three weeks. 2 4. Upon confronting the learned counsel for the petitioner with the aforesaid proposal, he expresses no objection and seeks disposal of the petition in these terms. 5. Accordingly, without delving into the merits of the case, this constitutional petition is disposed of with directions in the above terms, along with any pending application(s). JUDGE JUDGE Shafi
